description: Rework the voice of the remaining 13 docs pages (5 SDK reference, 5 server reference, 3 contributing) to have genuine personality in their prose sections. These pages are mostly tables and code — the voice changes are surgical, targeting only intros, section descriptions, and explanatory paragraphs. The personality should match the README's warmth without forcing humor into API tables.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Rework SDK reference pages (5 files) voice
userStories.description: Apply personality to the prose sections of the 5 SDK reference pages: plugin-class.mdx, tools.mdx, utilities.mdx, lifecycle-hooks.mdx, error-types.mdx. These are 90% code/tables — only touch the 10% that's prose.
acceptanceCriteria: plugin-class.mdx intro feels like 'here's everything your plugin needs to declare, all in one place'
tools.mdx intro conveys 'this is how your plugin talks to AI agents'
utilities.mdx intro conveys genuine 'I wrote these because the same boilerplate kept showing up' — with more personality than current
lifecycle-hooks.mdx intro explains hooks feel practical, not abstract
error-types.mdx intro conveys the design motivation with personality
All code blocks, tables, and MDX components preserved exactly — zero changes to non-prose content
No forced humor in API reference sections
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx (intro line 7: "Every OpenTabs plugin extends `OpenTabsPlugin` from the SDK. I put everything a plugin needs to declare..." — needs more personality)
- docs/content/docs/sdk/tools.mdx (intro line 7: "Tools are how your plugin exposes actions to AI agents." — correct but bland)
- docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx (intro line 7: "The SDK ships a set of utility functions... I wrote these to cut the boilerplate that kept showing up across plugins" — decent, could be warmer)
- docs/content/docs/sdk/lifecycle-hooks.mdx (intro line 7: "Lifecycle hooks let your plugin respond to adapter registration..." — needs personality)
- docs/content/docs/sdk/error-types.mdx (intro line 7: "ToolError is the structured error type... I designed it so that..." — decent, could be more personal)

Changes:
ONLY change intro paragraphs and section description prose. These files are 90% code blocks and tables. Do NOT touch:
- Any code blocks
- Any <Table> components
- Any import statements
- Any links
- Any function signatures
- Any parameter descriptions inside tables

Read the README at repo root for voice reference. The SDK reference should feel like a knowledgeable author explaining their API, not like auto-generated docs with 'I' sprinkled in.

Constraints:
- Be VERY surgical — only change prose paragraphs
- utilities.mdx is 997 lines, mostly tables — only change the ~10 prose paragraphs between table sections

userStories.title: Rework server reference pages (5 files) voice
userStories.description: Apply personality to the prose sections of the 5 server reference pages: browser-tools.mdx, mcp-server.mdx, configuration.mdx, cli.mdx, troubleshooting.mdx. These are heavily technical — only touch intros and explanatory prose.
acceptanceCriteria: browser-tools.mdx has a warm intro that conveys 'here are all the tools your agent gets for free'
mcp-server.mdx intro conveys 'it's just a localhost process, nothing fancy'
configuration.mdx intro feels like 'you shouldn't need to edit this often, but here's what's in there'
cli.mdx intro conveys personal pride in the CLI as a single entry point
troubleshooting.mdx advice feels like personal experience — 'this is what I do when things break'
All code blocks, tables, JSON examples, and MDX components preserved exactly
No forced humor in reference tables
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx (line 7: "These are all the built-in browser tools I expose to AI agents" — decent, could be warmer)
- docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (line 7: "Everything here describes the MCP server that `opentabs start` runs on your machine. It's a single localhost process — no cloud, no accounts." — good! just needs a tiny bit more)
- docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx (line 7: "All of your settings live in a single JSON file. You shouldn't need to edit it by hand very often" — good, could be slightly more personal)
- docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx (line 7: "I built the `opentabs` CLI to be your single entry point" — decent)
- docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx (line 9: "these two commands tell me most of what I need to know — they'll do the same for you" — good!)

Changes:
These files are 95% tables and code. ONLY change:
- The intro paragraph after frontmatter
- Brief section intro lines
- Explanatory prose between table/code sections

Do NOT touch:
- Any <Table> components or their contents
- Any code blocks
- Any JSON examples
- Any <ConfigDirectory />, <CliCommand /> components
- Any links

The troubleshooting page has the most prose opportunity — make diagnostic advice feel like personal experience.

Constraints:
- Be EXTREMELY surgical
- browser-tools.mdx and mcp-server.mdx are mostly tables — might only need 1-3 line changes each
- Read the README for voice reference

userStories.title: Rework contributing pages (3 files) voice
userStories.description: Apply deeper personality to the 3 contributing pages: architecture.mdx, dev-setup.mdx, publishing.mdx. These pages talk to potential contributors and should feel like a personal welcome to the codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: architecture.mdx design decisions feel like personal stories — 'this part was tricky', 'I chose X because Y'
dev-setup.mdx feels like personally onboarding someone — 'thanks for wanting to contribute, here's how to get going'
publishing.mdx feels practical and personal — 'here's how I publish releases'
The security section in architecture.mdx conveys genuine concern — 'this is the part I think about the most'
Quality checks in dev-setup.mdx are direct — 'run all of these, I mean all of them'
All code blocks, tables, and MDX components preserved exactly
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/contributing/architecture.mdx (496 lines — lots of tables but also substantial prose about design decisions)
- docs/content/docs/contributing/dev-setup.mdx (201 lines — setup walkthrough)
- docs/content/docs/contributing/publishing.mdx (127 lines — publish procedure)

Context:
These pages already have some first-person voice from the previous pass:
- architecture.mdx: "Let me walk you through how each one works and why I made the choices I did.", "This part was tricky.", "This is the part I think about the most."
- dev-setup.mdx: "Thanks for wanting to contribute. Let me get you set up."
- publishing.mdx: "I publish the platform packages as private packages to npm"

But compared to the README, the personality is still muted. The README has:
- "I know you're the kind of person who sets DANGEROUSLY_SKIP_PERMISSIONS=1"
- "This might sound a little wild: OpenTabs was built entirely by AI agents. Zero hand-written application code."
- "I won't pretend this is bulletproof."

The contributing pages need that level of honesty and personality in their design-decision prose. The architecture page especially — every 'I chose X' should feel like a genuine design story, not a polished explanation.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change wire protocol tables, endpoint tables, timeout tables
- Do NOT change code blocks
- Do NOT change <ArchitectureIllustration />, <MonorepoStructure />, <Table> components
- Focus on the prose between technical sections
- Read the README as gold standard
